title: Alternative Uses Test - brick (Guilford 1967)
prompt: List 8 unusual, non-obvious uses of a brick. Be concrete and specific. Avoid the standard everyday use (building walls or houses). Format: one use per line.
constraint_text: 8 unusual, non-obvious uses of a brick
must_avoid: the standard everyday use of a brick: building walls and houses
retrieval_set: use a brick as a paperweight
notes: Aspects=[] is intentional - AUT has no formal aspect dictionary. With v0.3's event-gated commit policy, when there are no aspects the BMR ΔF is 0 and vimarsa correctly does NOT fire, so the cascade commits the draft. revision_differs_from_draft is therefore not required here (the commit policy correctly chose draft); we measure n_distinct_uses to detect duplicate-listing collapse and require integrity_probe + no_leakage as for every Haiku subprocess call.
